hero.heading: Every service your car needs — in one Taree workshop.
hero.subtext: Logbook servicing to blue slips, AC regas to defect clearances. All makes, all models, all handled in-house at 16 Albert Lane.

bookingCta.heading: Not sure what you need? Just call.
bookingCta.subtext: Two minutes on the phone usually sorts it. (02) 6552 6131 — Mon–Fri 8:30am–4:30pm.

services.name: Logbook Servicing
services.summary: Warranty-safe servicing to manufacturer spec — Valvoline premium oils, stamped logbook, proper records.
services.body:
Under Australian Consumer Law you have the right to service your new car wherever you choose — you just need to follow the manufacturer's logbook schedule, using parts and fluids that meet or exceed the original specification. Miss a service or use the wrong oil, and the warranty can be voided.

Corney's does logbook servicing the right way. We follow the exact manufacturer schedule for your make, model and year. We use OEM-equivalent (or genuine) parts, and we fit Valvoline premium oils — the right grade for your vehicle, every time. We stamp your logbook and keep a digital record. Your new-car warranty stays completely intact, and you get honest pricing for quality work.

Toyota, Mazda, Ford, Hyundai, Kia, Mitsubishi, Nissan, Subaru, Holden, VW, European — all covered. Bring the logbook, we'll do the rest.

services.name: General Servicing & Repairs
services.summary: Routine servicing, mechanical repairs, and everything between — done right the first time.
services.body:
Regular servicing is the cheapest insurance you'll ever buy for your car. An $80 oil and filter change at the right interval prevents a $4,000 engine rebuild. Fresh spark plugs save real fuel. A clean air filter protects the engine.

We handle full servicing and mechanical repairs across every make and model — from the basic interval service through to timing belts, clutches, head gaskets and beyond. We tell you what's genuinely due, what can safely wait, and what's urgent. You only pay for work you've approved.

If we find something mid-job that wasn't in the quote, we stop and call you. Nothing gets done without your say-so.

services.name: Brakes
services.summary: Pads, rotors, callipers, fluid — diagnosed properly, priced fairly, back on the road same day where possible.
services.body:
Brakes are the one system you never compromise on. Warning signs: squealing, grinding, a soft or spongy pedal, vibration through the steering when you brake, pulling to one side, or a brake warning light. Any of those — book in this week.

We inspect, repair and replace the full brake system: pads, rotors (machined or replaced), callipers, flex hoses, brake lines and fluid. We fit quality, reputable brands — Bendix, Bosch, genuine — parts we'd happily put in our own vehicles. We'll show you the worn parts if you want to see them.

Most front-pad jobs are back to you same day. No driving away on brakes that aren't right.

services.name: Suspension & Steering
services.summary: Shocks, struts, ball joints, and wheel alignments — if it affects how your car handles, we fix it.
services.body:
A worn suspension doesn't just make for an uncomfortable ride — it affects your car's handling, tyre wear, and ability to brake in an emergency. Worn ball joints, broken springs, or tired shock absorbers can make a vehicle genuinely unsafe.

Signs you might need suspension or steering work: your car bounces excessively after a bump, you feel every pothole through the steering wheel, your tyres are wearing unevenly, or the car pulls to one side.

We inspect and replace shock absorbers, struts, ball joints, tie rod ends, CV joints, and steering components. We also perform wheel alignments to ensure your tyres are running true and your handling is spot-on.

services.name: Air Conditioning (ARCtick Licensed)
services.summary: $189 fixed-price regas. Leak tests from $88. ARCtick-licensed, right here in Taree.
services.body:
Under Australian law, handling automotive refrigerant requires an ARCtick licence. Corney's holds one — the right equipment, the right training, the right paperwork to do the job properly.

**Fixed-price AC regas: $189.** Standard passenger vehicle, all-inclusive. Proper recovery, evacuation and recharge — done to manufacturer specification. For non-standard vehicles, heavy-duty systems or R1234yf (newer refrigerant) we'll quote up front.

If your AC has lost gas, we find the leak first so the repair actually lasts. **Leak tests from $88** using UV dye or nitrogen pressure — we locate it, quote the fix, and regas once the system is sealed. That way the gas stays in.

We also handle full AC repairs: compressors, condensers, expansion valves, evaporators, blower motors, bad smells, weak airflow and climate-control faults. Whatever your AC is doing (or not doing), bring it in.

services.name: Batteries & Electrical
services.summary: Battery testing, replacement, and electrical fault diagnosis for all vehicles.
services.body:
Modern vehicles are heavily dependent on their electrical systems — and when something goes wrong, it's not always obvious what the cause is.

We handle everything from straightforward battery replacements through to tracking down intermittent electrical faults that have been driving you mad for months. Our diagnostic equipment lets us read your car's control systems to pinpoint exactly where the fault is — so we fix the actual problem the first time.

If your battery is flat, your alternator isn't charging, a sensor has failed, or there's a short somewhere in the system — bring it to us and we'll sort it.

services.name: Cooling Systems
services.summary: Radiator flushes, coolant leaks, thermostat replacement — keep your engine running at the right temperature.
services.body:
Your car's cooling system is what prevents the engine from overheating. A small coolant leak or a failing thermostat can quickly turn into a very expensive engine repair if it's not caught early.

Signs of cooling system problems: your temperature gauge is running higher than usual, you can smell coolant, there's steam from under the bonnet, or you're regularly topping up the coolant.

We service and repair radiators, coolant hoses, water pumps, thermostats, and cooling fans. We'll perform a cooling system flush and refill if it's been a while — old coolant becomes acidic and can corrode your system from the inside out.

services.name: Diagnostics
services.summary: Modern scan tools to identify exactly what's wrong — accurate diagnosis, accurate repair.
services.body:
When your check engine light comes on — or any other warning light — it means your car's computer has logged a fault code. The right diagnostic equipment reads that code and points us to the exact system that's triggered the warning.

Good diagnostics are the foundation of a good repair. Read the code, interpret it properly, and you fix the actual problem rather than chasing symptoms. That means less time in the workshop and a repair that lasts.

We use professional-grade scan tools that cover all makes and models, including European vehicles. Whether it's an engine management issue, a transmission fault, or an ABS/traction control warning — we can read it and fix it.

services.name: Pink Slips (eSafety Check)
services.summary: In plain English: a Pink Slip is the annual safety check you need to renew NSW rego on a vehicle 5+ years old.
services.body:
A Pink Slip (formally an eSafety inspection) is required every year when you renew NSW registration on any light vehicle older than 5 years. It confirms the vehicle meets minimum safety standards — brakes, tyres, steering, suspension, lights, wipers, seat belts, windscreen, exhaust and body condition.

We do them quickly and fairly. Most pink slips are 30–45 minutes. If your vehicle passes, we lodge the eS certificate electronically with Transport for NSW and you can renew your rego the same day.

If something fails, we explain exactly what — and quote the repair before touching anything. Our job is a safe, legal vehicle back on the road. You leave knowing exactly where you stand.

services.name: Blue Slips (AUVIS Inspection)
services.summary: In plain English: a Blue Slip is the deeper inspection you need to put an unregistered or interstate vehicle on NSW plates.
services.body:
A Blue Slip is required in NSW whenever you want to register a vehicle that isn't currently registered in the state — interstate vehicles, vehicles whose rego has lapsed, rebuilt vehicles, or anything that's never been on NSW plates.

It's more comprehensive than a pink slip. Blue slips are AUVIS (Authorised Unregistered Vehicle Inspection Scheme) inspections: we verify the vehicle's identity (VIN, compliance plates, engine number), confirm it meets NSW vehicle standards, and check it's mechanically safe for the road.

**Corney's is an authorised AUVIS station**, right here in Taree. Once we issue the Blue Slip, you take it to Service NSW to register. Not sure if you need a pink slip or a blue slip? Give us a call, two minutes will sort it.

services.name: Defect Clearances
services.summary: Got a defect notice from police or Transport for NSW? We repair and clear it so you're legal again.
services.body:
If you've been stopped by police or an RMS inspector and issued a defect notice, the listed defects need to be repaired and cleared by an authorised repairer before the vehicle is roadworthy again. Leave it unresolved and fines and rego cancellation follow.

Corney's is an authorised defect-clearance workshop. We work through the items on your notice — brakes, tyres, lights, exhaust, suspension, bodywork, modifications — repair what's needed, re-inspect the vehicle, and submit the clearance. Usually back on the road within days, often sooner.

If you've just been defected and don't know what to do, call us the same day. Priority booking for defect jobs.

services.name: Pre-Purchase Inspections
services.summary: Buying a used car? Let us check it over before you sign anything — it could save you thousands.
services.body:
Buying a used car is one of the biggest purchases most people make — and the risks are real. Hidden rust, accident damage, worn mechanicals, or a flood-affected vehicle can turn a bargain into a money pit very quickly.

A pre-purchase inspection by an independent mechanic is the best $100–$200 you can spend. We'll look over the car thoroughly — checking for rust, oil leaks, tyre condition, brake wear, suspension play, signs of previous accident damage, and anything else that's out of the ordinary.

At the end of the inspection, we'll give you a plain-English report on the car's condition, anything we'd fix before buying, and a rough estimate of what those repairs would cost. You go into the negotiation — or walk away — with full information.

services.name: 4WD & Fleet Vehicles
services.summary: From utes and 4WDs to small business fleets — keeping your work vehicles on the road.
services.body:
Mid North Coast NSW is 4WD country. Whether you're running a trade ute, a family 4WD, or a small fleet of work vehicles, keeping them serviced and reliable is non-negotiable.

We service and repair all 4WD and light commercial vehicles — including utes, vans, and SUVs from Toyota, Ford, Mitsubishi, Nissan, Isuzu, and more. We understand that downtime is money for a business, so we work to get your vehicles back to you as quickly as possible.

Fleet servicing: if you run multiple vehicles and want a single point of contact for your servicing needs, talk to us about a fleet arrangement. We'll work out a servicing schedule that keeps your vehicles on the road and your costs predictable.
